ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Proposed acquisition of Talaroo Digital Custody Pty Ltd by Kestrel Harbour Holdings Ltd (ASX: KHH)
the transaction is not generally available information
company trading blackout applies until market announcement
no personal dealing in KHH securities or relevant crypto instruments without clearance
no material cyber incident between signing and completion
AFSL variation for digital asset custody
no public marketing of these crypto/ESG products prior to ASX release
Non‑Binding Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Talaroo Digital Custody Pty Ltd by Kestrel Harbour Holdings Ltd (ASX: KHH).

Transaction: Buyer to acquire 100% of Talaroo Digital Custody Pty Ltd for AUD 48m upfront cash plus up to AUD 12m earn‑out over 24 months linked to AUM of tokenised climate certificates and PoS staking revenues.

Seller discloses an April 2026 intrusion attempt on a non‑production wallet monitor (contained, no client losses) and agrees to a 12‑month cyber breach indemnity with a AUD 5m cap and escrow of AUD 3m at completion.
Exclusivity and timing: Seller grants Buyer 45 days exclusivity; long‑stop date 30 September 2026; break fee AUD 1.2m payable by Seller for wilful breach of exclusivity.

must_detect.matched_text:
88 Quokka Street, Wangi NSW 2011; Contact: Mr. Elias Morton (CFO), elias.morton@kestrelharbour.com.au.
Seller: Warru Capital Pty Ltd as trustee for the Talaroo HoldCo Trust; Contact: Ms. Priya Jandekar (Director), priya.j@warrucapital.com.au.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 100% of Talaroo Digital Custody Pty Ltd for AUD 48m upfront cash plus up to AUD 12m earn‑out over 24 months linked to AUM of tokenised climate certificates and PoS staking revenues.
Conditions precedent: satisfactory legal/financial/cyber due diligence; no material cyber incident between signing and completion; ASX/ASIC consultation, AFSL variation for digital asset custody, AUSTRAC registration maintained, and FIRB approval if required.
Due diligence scope includes review of private key management, hot/cold wallet segregation, incident logs, and assurance over the “Ganaroo Carbon Unit” tokenisation platform’s emissions data integrity.
Seller discloses an April 2026 intrusion attempt on a non‑production wallet monitor (contained, no client losses) and agrees to a 12‑month cyber breach indemnity with a AUD 5m cap and escrow of AUD 3m at completion.
Disclosure and announcement: the transaction is not generally available information; no selective disclosure; any announcement to be coordinated under ASX LR 3.1 and Corporations Act s674 with pre‑briefing to ASX as needed.
Information management: maintain an Insider List for this deal and restrict access behind information barriers; persons on the list include Mr. Elias Morton, Dr. Saffron Yue, and Ms. Priya Jandekar; company trading blackout applies until market announcement.
Tipping and dealing: directors, officers, and staff are reminded of Corporations Act s1043A (insider trading) and the Company Securities Trading Policy; no personal dealing in KHH securities or relevant crypto instruments without clearance.
Commercial integration: post‑completion, Talaroo to pilot custody of “GreenBay Energy Tokens” for corporate clients and to resell climate data analytics; no public marketing of these crypto/ESG products prior to ASX release.
Governance and people: on completion, Dr. Saffron Yue
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
Non‑Binding Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Talaroo Digital Custody Pty Ltd by Kestrel Harbour Holdings Ltd (ASX: KHH).
Buyer: Kestrel Harbour Holdings Ltd, Level 22, 88 Quokka Street, Wangi NSW 2011; Contact: Mr. Elias Morton (CFO), elias.morton@kestrelharbour.com.au.
Seller: Warru Capital Pty Ltd as trustee for the Talaroo HoldCo Trust; Contact: Ms. Priya Jandekar (Director), priya.j@warrucapital.com.au.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 100% of Talaroo Digital Custody Pty Ltd for AUD 48m upfront cash plus up to AUD 12m earn‑out over 24 months linked to AUM of tokenised climate certificates and PoS staking revenues.
Conditions precedent: satisfactory legal/financial/cyber due diligence; no material cyber incident between signing and completion; ASX/ASIC consultation, AFSL variation for digital asset custody, AUSTRAC registration maintained, and FIRB approval if required.
Due diligence scope includes review of private key management, hot/cold wallet segregation, incident logs, and assurance over the “Ganaroo Carbon Unit” tokenisation platform’s emissions data integrity.
Seller discloses an April 2026 intrusion attempt on a non‑production wallet monitor (contained, no client losses) and agrees to a 12‑month cyber breach indemnity with a AUD 5m cap and escrow of AUD 3m at completion.
Disclosure and announcement: the transaction is not generally available information; no selective disclosure; any announcement to be coordinated under ASX LR 3.1 and Corporations Act s674 with pre‑briefing to ASX as needed.
Information management: maintain an Insider List for this deal and restrict access behind information barriers; persons on the list include Mr. Elias Morton, Dr. Saffron Yue, and Ms. Priya Jandekar; company trading blackout applies until market announcement.
Tipping and dealing: directors, officers, and staff are reminded of Corporations Act s1043A (insider trading) and the Company Securities Trading Policy; no personal dealing in KHH securities or relevant crypto instruments without clearance.
Commercial integration: post‑completion, Talaroo to pilot custody of “GreenBay Energy Tokens” for corporate clients and to resell climate data analytics; no public marketing of these crypto/ESG products prior to ASX release.
Governance and people: on completion, Dr. Saffron Yue will be appointed Head of Digital Custody reporting to the CFO; Seller principals to enter 12‑month non‑compete and provide transition services.
Exclusivity and timing: Seller grants Buyer 45 days exclusivity; long‑stop date 30 September 2026; break fee AUD 1.2m payable by Seller for wilful breach of exclusivity.
